key_evidence: ~15,000 people in Borno State (Dikwa, Kaga, Kalabalge) at risk of IPC/CH Phase 5 Catastrophe during the June-August 2026 lean season (FAO-WFP).
~34.8M Nigerians projected at CH Phase 3+, including ~1.8M in Emergency (Phase 4); Nigeria added to the highest-concern FAO-WFP hunger-hotspot tier.
IRC characterizes the famine risk as 'entirely man-made' and 'entirely preventable'; access is the central barrier.

Recurring decade-plus northeast crisis indicates structural and accountability failure, not a one-off shock; the move crosses the Developing-to-Critical band boundary despite a sub-5-point composite delta.

evidence.quote: In certain highly localized areas, particularly in Borno State in Nigeria (Dikwa, Kaga, and Kalabalge), more than 15,000 people are at risk of slipping into Catastrophe (phase 5).
evidence.claim: ~15,000 people in Borno State at risk of IPC/CH Phase 5 Catastrophe in June-August 2026
evidence.source: FAO-WFP (Hunger Hotspots, West Africa and the Sahel)

evidence.quote: This looming famine is entirely man-made.
evidence.claim: The Nigeria famine risk is preventable and attributable to human action/inaction, not natural causes
